Name: Backend routes and services
Scope: backend/ routes, services, query helpers (createFinishedPlan cplx 72, devices-updates/db, arcgis.ts)
Approach: One route or service cluster per PR; extract validators and query builders.

Name: Nabewerking flows
Approach: Split large components; extract PDF/coords helpers; one screen per PR.
RisksIfDoneWrong: PDF layout and coordinate edit regressions

Name: Voorbereiding wizards
Approach: Finish duplication first; then split remaining oversized units.

Name: Map shell UI
Approach: Extract layer config tables and map init steps; dedicated map QA pass.

Name: ArcGIS helpers
Approach: Split graphic builders and geometry utilities; keep pure functions testable.
Verification: Map smoke + unit tests on pure helpers

Name: Admin standalone pages
Approach: Extract subcomponents and data hooks per page.

Name: Timeslider feature
Approach: Reduce component complexity; extract data hooks.

Name: Frontend catch-all
Scope: Tools, Bottom lists, Common UI, hooks (non god), api-hooks, utils — slice per sub-area
Approach: One sub-area per PR: Tools, Bottom, hooks, Common (see MAINT-ARCH-PLAN.md).

Name: High fan-in hooks
Approach: Only with dedicated initiative; split logging vs content if needed.

Name: Module coupling
Scope: fetchApi, classNames, routeResponses, shared utils with high fan-in
Approach: Accept some fan-in on true infrastructure; avoid fake splits.

Name: Component independence
Scope: Hook and api-hook interface modules (hover-click-handlers, features, api-hooks)
Approach: Introduce feature-scoped hooks; thin wrappers over time.
RisksIfDoneWrong: Temporary architecture score dip when adding modules

Name: Component entanglement
Scope: HomePage ↔ hooks density; Timeslider transitive deps
Approach: Long-term boundary work; not a single PR.
